Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 2:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

I don't think "%p" will give you what is wanted, since newer kernels will "munge" this pointer when printing it.  At a minimum the patch should be updated to use "%pK" so that the actual pointer is printed.

I guess the expectation here is that printing the blocking lock pointer can be used to find some other message in the debug logs that shows the actual lock detail?
Removed reviewer Patrick Farrell.

This change cannot be cherry-picked to master.
Please rebase the change locally and upload the rebased commit for review.

commit.subject: LU-16285 ldlm: output the conflict lock
commit.message:
LU-16285 ldlm: output the conflict lock

Print out the detail info while lock BL timout
so we know which lock conflict with it.

Signed-off-by: Yang Sheng <ys@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: I5698341a694d64aabea0f28f97216ccbd57f5641
